title: Marcus Miller: Thoughts on Miles
subTitle: In the 2009 portrait of Marcus Miller, Miller looks back on his relationship with Miles Davis; he shares stories of the recording process of the album "Tutu," explaining how he wrote the title track.

title: Thelonious Monk: Jazz Greats
subTitle: The idiosyncratic pianist and composer Thelonious Monk in a performance with his quartet in Copenhagen, Denmark on April 17, 1966 and Warsaw, Poland on April 4, 1966.

title: B.B. King Orchestra - The North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1979, legendary American blues guitarist B.B. King gave an unforgettable performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, Netherlands.

title: Jazz Samba: Baden Powell, Ernesto Ribeiro
subTitle: Recorded in 1971 in Paris, Brazilian, classically trained bossa nova guitarist Baden Powell and his quartet play various compositions, including some originals.

title: Christian McBride Quartet at Malta 2018
subTitle: Double bassist Christian McBride presents his band New Jawn at the Malta Jazz Festival on July 20, 2018; hailing from Philadelphia, this musician combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical.

title: Jacques Loussier in Lepzig: Play Bach and More
subTitle: On July 28th 2004, the day of Bach's 254th anniversary of death, the Jacques Loussier Trio perform a special concert at the famous St. Thomas Church of Leipzig.

title: Fannie Mae
subTitle: Jaco Pastorius, a true musical innovator, takes the stage at the Montreal Jazz Festival; he is transforming the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument, wowing the audience in Canada with his unique style and talent.

title: Blue Note Festival: Gregory Porter, Chip Crawford, Aaron James & Emanuel Harrold
subTitle: Listening to Gregory Porter's burnished baritone.

title: Charles Mingus and Eric Dolphy Live in Liège
subTitle: Charles Mingus showcases a concert performed in April 1965 featuring Jaki Byard on piano, Dannie Richmond on drums, Johnny Coles on trumpet, Clifford Jordan on tenor sax and Eric Dolphy on alto sax, flute and bass clarinet.

title: Lianne La Havas: Live at Casino de Paris
subTitle: Lianne La Havas' performance at Casino de Paris shows the British singer-songwriter at her best; this concert is part of La Havas' "Blood Tour", promoting her second album "Blood."

title: George Shearing Duo feat. Neil Swainson
subTitle: British pianist George Shearing was equally at home on the classical concert stage as in jazz clubs; Shearing plays originals in a duo format with Canadian double bassist Neil Swainson.

title: Tribute to Monk
subTitle: In 2017, French pianist Laurent de Wilde performed his tribute to jazz icon Thelonious Monk at Jazz Ã Vienne.

title: Dizzy Gillespie - Jazz Greats
subTitle: American jazz trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie was an influential figure of bebop known for fusing Cuban, Latin American, and Carribean influences in his music.

title: The Maynard Ferguson Big Band
subTitle: The Maynard Ferguson Big Band's Winter Tour of 1980 included a stop at the Brussels Jazz Club; Ferguson and his band perform some hits of the day.

title: Blue Note Festival: Gregory Porter, Chip Crawford, Aaron James & Emanuel Harrold
subTitle: Listening to Gregory Porter's burnished baritone.

title: Charles Mingus and Eric Dolphy Live in Liège
subTitle: Charles Mingus showcases a concert performed in April 1965 featuring Jaki Byard on piano, Dannie Richmond on drums, Johnny Coles on trumpet, Clifford Jordan on tenor sax and Eric Dolphy on alto sax, flute and bass clarinet.

title: Lianne La Havas: Live at Casino de Paris
subTitle: Lianne La Havas' performance at Casino de Paris shows the British singer-songwriter at her best; this concert is part of La Havas' "Blood Tour", promoting her second album "Blood."

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: Back in 1982, American saxophonist Gerry Mulligan gave an unforgettable performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival; Mulligan was one of the leading arrangers in the era of cool jazz working with Miles Davis, Stan Kenton and Claude Thornhill.

title: Jose James
subTitle: Jose James is discovered by the DJ and radio broadcaster Gilles Peterson; joined on stage by a group of talented musicians, the singer is one of the prime examples of the direction jazz is heading towards.

title: Thelonious Monk: Jazz Greats
subTitle: The idiosyncratic pianist and composer Thelonious Monk in a performance with his quartet in Copenhagen, Denmark on April 17, 1966 and Warsaw, Poland on April 4, 1966.

title: Earl Hines Quartet: Spa, 1977
subTitle: Earl Hines and his group, consisting of Rudy Rutherford, Jimmy Leary, and Eddie Graham perform in Spa in 1977; singer Marva Josie joins the group for two songs before the set closes.

title: Monty Alexander Quartet -- North Sea Jazz
subTitle: On July 13, 1979, Jamaican pianist Monty Alexander was joined by bassist Steve Rodby, Frank Gant, Bobby Thomas and Othello Molineaux at the "North Sea Jazz Festival" in The Hague.

title: The Blue Note Masters -- Ron Carter, Bobby Hutcherson, Javon Jackson & Joe Chambers
subTitle: Explore how Ron Carter, Bobby Hutcherson and Joe Chambers establish themselves as forward-thinking and inventive band leaders, composers and studio musicians.

title: Jimmy Witherspoon Quartet & Rozaa Wortham 1985
subTitle: Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music; Jimmy Witherspoon ends the show with some lean-back blues.

title: Kenny Barron at Lviv Ukraine 2019
subTitle: Pianist Kenny Barron's appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine's Lviv is a celebration of jazz virtuosity.

title: Blue Note Festival: Gregory Porter, Chip Crawford, Aaron James & Emanuel Harrold
subTitle: Listening to Gregory Porter's burnished baritone.

title: Blue Note Festival: Gregory Porter, Chip Crawford, Aaron James & Emanuel Harrold
subTitle: Listening to Gregory Porter's burnished baritone.

title: Charles Mingus and Eric Dolphy Live in Liège
subTitle: Charles Mingus showcases a concert performed in April 1965 featuring Jaki Byard on piano, Dannie Richmond on drums, Johnny Coles on trumpet, Clifford Jordan on tenor sax and Eric Dolphy on alto sax, flute and bass clarinet.

title: Lianne La Havas: Live at Casino de Paris
subTitle: Lianne La Havas' performance at Casino de Paris shows the British singer-songwriter at her best; this concert is part of La Havas' "Blood Tour", promoting her second album "Blood."

title: Art Blakey & The Jazz Messengers; Olympia, Paris: Art Blakey, Wayne Shorter, Freddie Hubbard & Curtis Fuller
subTitle: Art Blakey's Jazz Messengers performs in Olympia Hall in Paris, France on March 16, 1963.

title: Münchner Klaviersommer: Eliane Elias Trio
subTitle: In this live concert at the Philharmonie in Munich, Eliane Elias shows her distinctive and immediately recognizable musical style, which blends her Brazilian samba roots and voice with her instrumental bebop, jazz and compositional skills.

title: Dizzy Gillespie - Jazz Greats
subTitle: American jazz trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie was an influential figure of bebop known for fusing Cuban, Latin American, and Carribean influences in his music.

title: 'The Divine One' in 1958 - Sarah Vaughan, Richard Davis, Ronnell Bright and Art Morgan
subTitle: Renditions of songs by Harold Arlen, Leonard Bernstein, Johnny Burke, the Gershwins, and Stephen Sondheim are pure diva magic.

title: Jazzmeia Horn at Malta Jazz Festival
subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: Gilberto Gil: Jazz à Vienne
subTitle: Brazilian artist Gilberto Gil returns to Jazz à Vienne in France, with a new round of singing that is inspired by his latest album; Gil, known as the Brazilian minister of Culture, is one of the pioneers of the cultural movement "tropicalismo."

title: Chucho Valdes at Bata Jazz Malta 2019
subTitle: Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brings his band Jazz Batá Two to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019; they deliver a performance that reverberates through the historic island.

title: Larry Graham: Live in Paris
subTitle: Larry Graham and Graham Central Station perform at the Trianon in Paris, where 600 attendees watch the renowned bass player. Graham, a pioneer of slap-pop electric bass, shows his skill.

title: Maybe This Time
subTitle: The recording captures Tony Bennett's performance at London's Royal Albert Hall on March 2, 1974, showcasing his vocal skill and charisma.

title: Quincy Jones: 'Q' conducting his 'Dream Band'
subTitle: Jazz Icons: Quincy Jones'spotlights a young 'Q' conducting his 'dream band'-an 18-piece orchestra of world-renowned players such as Clark Terry, Phil Woods, Sahib Shihab, Budd Johnson and Benny Bailey. This recording features 5 songs and is one of the finest examples of big band jazz ever to be captured on film. Shot in Belgium in 1961, this concert is one of the only known visual documents of this legendary tour.

title: Dhafer Youssef -- Jazz à Vienne 2018
subTitle: In 2018, Tunisian composer Dhafer Youssef alternated between his voice and oud playing at Jazz à Vienne; his abilities have to be heard to be believed; he is joined by bassist Matt Brewer and drummer Aaron Parks.

title: Chucho Valdes at Bata Jazz Malta 2019
subTitle: Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brings his band Jazz Batá Two to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019; they deliver a performance that reverberates through the historic island.

title: Don Cherry's Multikulti
subTitle: Cherry and his quartet perform in Stuttgart, Germany.

title: Big Joe Turner, Kenny Clarke Trio, Art Simmons Trio
subTitle: Performances of pianist Joe Turner, the Kenny Clarke Trio and the Art Simmons Trio.

title: Gilberto Gil: Jazz à Vienne
subTitle: Brazilian artist Gilberto Gil returns to Jazz à Vienne in France, with a new round of singing that is inspired by his latest album; Gil, known as the Brazilian minister of Culture, is one of the pioneers of the cultural movement "tropicalismo."

title: Chucho Valdes at Bata Jazz Malta 2019
subTitle: Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brings his band Jazz Batá Two to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019; they deliver a performance that reverberates through the historic island.

title: Jazz à Ramatuelle
subTitle: Italian jazz pianist Antonio Faraò.

title: The Great Charlie Galbraith All Star Jazz Band
subTitle: In 1963, the small Belgian town of Comblain-la-Tour hosted the talented groups of Charlie Galbraith, Knut Kiesewetter, Bud Shank and Pim Jacobs and Marc Laferrière in a jazz festival that mixed new and old styles.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosts the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

title: Bandit 65 - Jazzfest Bonn
subTitle: Bandit 65 is the breathtakingly virtuoso trio of guitarists Kurt Rosenwinkel and Tim Motzer with percussionist Gintas Janusonis. With great power and astounding beauty they explore the intersection of rhythm and soundscape in free improvisations.

title: Smith, Adderley, Brubeck and Mingus at Newport Jazz Festival
subTitle: Four masterful groups consist of alto saxophonist Cannonball Adderley and his quintet, Hammond organist Jimmy Smith and his band and pianist Dave Brubeck and his trio.

title: John Lee Hooker Live in Paris
subTitle: American blues singer and guitarist John Lee Hooker is joined by Carey Bell on harmonica, Joe Harper on bass, Lester Dorsie on drums and Jimmy Dawkins on electric guitar in this 1970 performance from Maison de la Radio in Paris, France.

title: Gilberto Gil: Jazz à Vienne
subTitle: Brazilian artist Gilberto Gil returns to Jazz à Vienne in France, with a new round of singing that is inspired by his latest album; Gil, known as the Brazilian minister of Culture, is one of the pioneers of the cultural movement "tropicalismo."

title: Gilberto Gil: Jazz à Vienne
subTitle: Brazilian artist Gilberto Gil returns to Jazz à Vienne in France, with a new round of singing that is inspired by his latest album; Gil, known as the Brazilian minister of Culture, is one of the pioneers of the cultural movement "Tropicalismo."

title: Chucho Valdes at Bata Jazz Malta 2019
subTitle: Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brings his band Jazz Batá Two to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019; they deliver a performance that reverberates through the historic island.

title: Jazz à Ramatuelle
subTitle: Italian jazz pianist Antonio Faraò.

title: Astrud Gilberto
subTitle: In 1982, Dutch big band The Skymasters accompanied monumental jazz trumpet legend Dizzy Gillespie and the Brazilian bossa nova singer Astrud Gilberto at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague.

title: Just in Time
subTitle: Experience Tony Bennett in London's Royal Albert Hall on Mar. 2, 1974; the performance showcases Bennett's vocal prowess and charisma.

title: Soul Makossa in Stuttgart - Manu Dibango, Daniel Rallo, Jerry Malekani & Frederic Gaillardet
subTitle: Manu Dibango performs live.

title: Münchner Klaviersommer: Weather Update
subTitle: Jazz-fusion band Weather Update, founded by Austrian keyboardist Joe Zawinul, performs at Münchner Klaviersommer, in Munich, Germany in 1986.

title: Jazzmeia Horn at Malta Jazz Festival
subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: Fly Me to the Moon: Curtis Stigers
subTitle: The Danish Radio Big Band and Curtis Stigers celebrate Frank Sinatra's concerts at the Sands Hotel.

title: Jazzmeia Horn at Malta Jazz Festival
subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: Seun Kuti & Egypt 80'
subTitle: Seun Anikulapo Kuti possesses the grace, energy and fury of his father, the legendary artist Fela Kuti; with the musicians of Fela Kuti's legendary band Egypt 80, Seaun Kuti brings to life the most original incarnation of Afrobeat.

title: Estival 1986: Jon Hendricks, Judith Hendricks, Kim Lindsay & Mart Ledford
subTitle: John Carl Hendricks is one of the instigators of vocalese, an art form that aims to add lyrics to existing instrumental pieces and replace several instruments with singing.

title: The Story of Jazz
subTitle: Performances, commentary and compelling historic insights provide an overview of the cross-cultural influences that produced the constantly evolving sounds of jazz music.

title: Münchner Klaviersommer: Weather Update
subTitle: Jazz-fusion band Weather Update, founded by Austrian keyboardist Joe Zawinul, performs at Münchner Klaviersommer, in Munich, Germany in 1986.

title: Jazzmeia Horn at Malta Jazz Festival
subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Walter Trout, who was born in New Jersey and is based in Los Angeles, is a world-famous contemporary blues rock guitarist and vocalist; the Walter Trout Band performs at the 50th Burghausen International Jazz Week in 2019.

title: Avishai Cohen at Lviv Ukraine 2017
subTitle: Avishai Cohen's trio with Noam David and Omri Mor join forces with the International Symphony Orchestra INSO-Lviv, conducted by Christian Schumann.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1981, American singer and Grammy Award winner Al Jarreau gives a performance for the audience of the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, the Netherlands.

title: Time After Time
subTitle: This selection of performances by some of the iconic and influential male jazz and pop vocalists in history showcases these legends' unique essence.

title: James Brown : The North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1981, the legendary American singer James Brown gave an unforgettable performance to the audience at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, the Netherlands.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Chucho Valdes and his Afro-Cuban messengers are the prime example of Latin-inspired jazz music; during his performance the pianist brings the streets of Havana to the Jazz a Vienne stage.

title: Fine and Mellow
subTitle: This compilation features recordings from the 1950s of Billie Holiday singing a string of her successes; enjoy this unique footage of one of jazz's all-time greats!

title: The Story of Jazz
subTitle: Performances, commentary and compelling historic insights provide an overview of the cross-cultural influences that produced the constantly evolving sounds of jazz music.

subTitle: Jazz-fusion band Weather Update, founded by Austrian keyboardist Joe Zawinul, performs at Münchner Klaviersommer, in Munich, Germany in 1986.
